sanskrit:
सद्भावे साधुभावे च सदित्येतत्प्रयुज्यते।
प्रशस्ते कर्मणि तथा सच्छब्दः पार्थ युज्यते।।
यज्ञे तपसि दाने च स्थितिः सदिति चोच्यते।
कर्म चैव तदर्थीयं सदित्येवाभिधीयते।।
transliteration: sad-bhāve sādhu-bhāve ca sad ity etat prayujyate | praśaste karmaṇi tathā sac-chabdaḥ pārtha yujyate || yajñe tapasi dāne ca sthitiḥ sad iti cocyate | karma caiva tad-arthīyaṁ sad ity evābhidhīyate ||
literal_meaning: In the sense of the nature of the Absolute, and in the sense of the nature of goodness, the word “sat” is employed; so too for praiseworthy actions, O Pārtha, the sound “sat” is used. Steadiness in sacrifice, austerity and charity is designated by the word “sat”; and any work performed for the sake of That is also called “sat.”
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that the syllable “sat” is the psychological anchor that turns every action—whether ritual, self-discipline or service—into an act of alignment with the True Self. When actions are tagged with “sat,” the mind reframes them as offerings rather than egoic achievements, reducing performance anxiety and increasing intrinsic motivation.
psychological_state: Post-Decisional Commitment—Arjuna has chosen to fight, now needs a symbolic device to keep actions congruent with his higher values under fire.
trigger_scenario: Before hitting “send” on a high-stakes email, silently whispering “This is for the team” to shift from self-threat to service mindset.
anchor_text: Label every deed with 'true' and watch ego dissolve into steady purpose.
